prose:
Frontier-orbital calculation on formaldehyde (H₂C=O, 4 atoms: O, C, H, H) using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard', density-fit RI, PySCF RKS), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: user-supplied .xyz file at /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz — single-point, no re-optimisation at this level. SCF converged in 8 cycles. No warnings reported (clean run). Integrity status: trustworthy = true, all checks passed.

Key results (Koopmans-theorem, orbital energy differences):
  HOMO energy      = −7.6523067 eV
  LUMO energy      = −1.7826355 eV
  HOMO–LUMO gap    =  5.8696712 eV  ← headline value
  Total energy     = −114.55325 Hartree (−3117.1528 eV)

Koopmans descriptors:
  Vertical IP            = 7.6523067 eV
  Vertical EA            = 1.7826355 eV
  Electronegativity (χ)  = 4.7174711 eV
  Chemical hardness (η)  = 2.9348356 eV
  Chemical softness (σ)  = 0.34073459 eV⁻¹
  Electrophilicity (ω)   = 3.7914447 eV

Caveats: (1) Single-point on supplied geometry — not re-optimised at B3LYP/def2-TZVP. (2) Koopmans-theorem IP/EA are frozen-orbital estimates, not ΔSCF values. (3) The HOMO–LUMO gap is a ground-state KS orbital energy difference, not an optical/TD-DFT excitation energy.

CLI invocation:
  /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier \
    --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard \
    /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz \
    --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmplfwog1xd/live.json
